summary.intent: Fix Gossamer's UI loading screen (stuck spinner) and ensure the indexer picks up new local commits from the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch without hanging on SSH prompts.
summary.outcome: The API server hang was traced to git fetch blocking the event loop; fetch was made async with a timeout. The poll loop was fixed to reset to the local branch tip (refs/heads/) instead of origin. A force-push was needed to recover from an accidental bad commit during debugging. The root cause of missing checkpoints was the entire post-commit hook silently failing and rolling back local commits on push failure.

repo: Gossamer is an Electron app (app/) + a separate Node HTTP/WS API server (src/serve.ts + src/server.ts). Both must run concurrently — the UI polls http://localhost:3000/api/sessions.
The entire CLI writes checkpoint data to the branch entire/checkpoints/v1 via a post-commit hook (entire hooks git post-commit). It rolls back local commits if the push to origin fails.
Session checkpoints only exist on entire/checkpoints/v1 after a session is finalized. In-progress sessions live on shadow branches (entire/<short-sha>-<hash>).
The gossamer indexer uses a git worktree at .gossamer/checkpoints. The worktree having entire/checkpoints/v1 checked out causes 'branch locked' conflicts when entire tries to push.
The post-commit hook suppresses all errors with 2>/dev/null || true, making silent failures invisible.
Session 7d0ed455 had commits on its shadow branch but no checkpoint on entire/checkpoints/v1 because the hook had been silently failing on earlier commits.

code.finding: Originally used execSync for git fetch inside the async runIndex polling loop, blocking the entire Node.js event loop and preventing HTTP requests from being served while git hung.

merge --ff-only was used to pull origin into the local branch during polling, which caused gossamer to advance refs/heads/entire/checkpoints/v1 to match origin — including bad commits. Removed; the worktree now only resets to the local branch tip using refs/heads/ prefix.
GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0, BatchMode=yes, and SSH_AUTH_SOCK clearing do not prevent macOS Keychain GUI SSH dialogs — macOS SSH agent bypasses all of these.

workflow: On macOS, GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 and SSH BatchMode=yes only suppress terminal prompts; the macOS Keychain SSH agent shows GUI dialogs regardless.
git reset --hard <branch> without the refs/heads/ prefix can silently resolve to origin/<branch> when the remote tracking ref is checked out in a worktree context.
Running git operations in a worktree that tracks a remote branch triggers background auto-fetch even without explicit fetch calls.
Testing git push from a worktree with staged changes causes those changes to be included in the push — always check git status before testing push behavior in a worktree.

friction: Multiple rounds of SSH suppression attempts (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT, BatchMode, SSH_AUTH_SOCK, --no-track) all failed because macOS Keychain bypasses them — eventually abandoned in favor of assuming proper ssh-agent setup.
The post-commit hook's 2>/dev/null || true masked the real failure cause throughout the entire debug session.
An accidental force-push of a bad commit (deleting checkpoint files) occurred during debugging due to staged changes in the worktree.
The merge --ff-only in the poll loop silently advanced the local branch to a bad origin commit, compounding the accidental push.
Several reflog digs were needed to understand that entire itself was reverting local commits after push failure.
git reset without refs/heads/ prefix silently resolved to origin, causing confusion about why new local commits weren't being picked up.
open_items: Investigate why the entire post-commit hook was silently failing on earlier commits for session 7d0ed455 — checkpoint 18f992950c10 referenced in commit 35f7367 was never written.
The gossamer worktree holding entire/checkpoints/v1 may cause 'branch locked' errors when entire tries to push — needs verification.
The npm start script now uses concurrently to run both serve and app, but the app script added to package.json root was an oversight fix — confirm it's the right entry point.
Consider removing 2>/dev/null || true from the post-commit hook so failures are visible during development.
The fetch in runIndex still runs on every poll interval even though gossamer no longer uses the result to advance the local branch — could be removed or made conditional.
